title: Pennsylvania OAG mirror page on AI Biden robocall warning letter
procedural_posture: institutional warning and public response summary
source_depth: public page with linked official PDF
strongest_safe_claim: A publicly reachable state-attorney-general mirror confirms a multistate warning response to AI-generated robocalls impersonating President Biden.
overread_to_avoid: Do not present the mirror page as a final adjudication, a prevalence signal, or a substitute for the official PDF when quoting posture details.

title: State AG Task Force warning letter to Life Corporation
strongest_safe_claim: The local shard can cite exact document-grade wording that Life Corp appeared to have originated an artificially generated robocall campaign and that the calls may violate federal and state law.

next_stronger_source_or_move: Add one direct state-origin or later-case procedural document before claiming fuller state-case chronology.

title: FTC proposes new protections to combat AI impersonation of individuals
strongest_safe_claim: The FTC publicly framed AI-generated deepfakes as part of the impersonation-fraud problem in early 2024.
overread_to_avoid: Do not describe this source as a final rule, enforcement outcome, or case-specific liability finding.
visualization_hook: Federal-policy node for false-authorship and provenance-confusion views
next_stronger_source_or_move: If needed later, pair with final-rule or enforcement material rather than treating proposal text as endpoint evidence.

title: FCC Declaratory Ruling FCC 24-17
strongest_safe_claim: The FCC directly states that TCPA restrictions on artificial or prerecorded voice encompass current AI technologies that generate human voices.
overread_to_avoid: Do not broaden this into a generalized claim that all AI voice uses are illegal outside the quoted robocall framework.
visualization_hook: Federal legal-anchor badge for synthetic-voice governance cluster
next_stronger_source_or_move: Use as a framework anchor; stronger case-level claims still need direct incident or disposition records.

title: FCC Notice of Apparent Liability FCC 24-60
strongest_safe_claim: The FCC enforcement-stage document ties spoofed deepfake robocalls, STIR/SHAKEN attestation posture, and New Hampshire election targeting into one reviewer-citable federal record.
overread_to_avoid: Do not present notice-of-apparent-liability language as a final penalty, merits finding, or universal telecom-practice conclusion.
visualization_hook: Telecom-authentication edge connecting provenance confusion to election-misinformation and caller-attribution views
next_stronger_source_or_move: If available later, pair with settlement/final order or direct state-origin text.
